phase-scope.phase-27: Foundation only: pending registration primitives, token hashing, TTL/replay protection, email risk checks, and compatibility evidence while active /register stays unchanged.
phase-scope.phase-28: Cutover: /register must return pending verification state and must not create active KrotPN access before email ownership is proven.

verification-levels.module-local: Risk-check and pending-token tests run without live email provider, DNS mutability, VPN provisioning, or MTProto runtime.
verification-levels.wave-level: Phase-27 must be verified together with M-040 email delivery fake-provider tests.
verification-levels.phase-level: Existing active /register behavior is a protected compatibility boundary until Phase-28.

module-checks.check-1: Allowed email input normalizes consistently and creates a pending verification record without issuing JWT, trial, VPN, referral, or MTProto side effects.
module-checks.check-2: Verification tokens are stored hashed or otherwise non-recoverable; plaintext tokens never persist.
module-checks.check-3: Expired, replayed, malformed, duplicate, and blocked-domain paths are rejected safely.
module-checks.check-4: Temporary/disposable and configured blocked domains are rejected before M-040 email sending is called.
module-checks.check-5: Allowlist policy can override broad deny rules for operator-approved domains.
module-checks.check-6: Phase-27 does not wire the new pending-registration flow into active /register yet.

phase-28-cutover-gates.gate-1: Unverified /register no longer creates active user access or JWT tokens.
phase-28-cutover-gates.gate-2: Verified activation creates user, trial, VPN device, VPN client, referral effects, and later MTProto assignment exactly once.
phase-28-cutover-gates.gate-3: Verification token replay cannot duplicate trial, referral, VPN, or MTProto side effects.

scenarios.scenario-1: User submits allowed email to the pending registration service and receives safe check-email state.
scenarios.scenario-2: Temporary email domain is blocked before verification email is sent.
scenarios.scenario-3: Malformed, expired, replayed, and duplicate pending-registration tokens fail with safe reasons.
scenarios.scenario-4: Pending registration path cannot emit JWT tokens or provision KrotPN resources in Phase-27.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Mask email local parts in logs and never log verification tokens, token hashes, password values, or raw risk-provider payloads.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Failure reasons may be operator-readable but must not expose whether an unrelated account exists.

autonomous-execution-safety.reason: Phase-27 is safe for local execution if tests prove no /register cutover, no deploy/install edits, no live email provider calls, and no VPN/MTProto side effects.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-13: backend/tests/test_email_verification_guard.py passed locally, covering disposable-domain blocking before email send, allowlist override, env domain parsing, HMAC token storage, one-time consume, expiry, zero user side effects, and active /register compatibility boundary.
